user_title: Head of Change Execution, WPB Private Banking & Wealth Solutions, Asia Pacific

talking_points.headline: McKinsey Confirms AI Existential Threat — Change Execution Must Rewire, Not Optimise
talking_points.why_it_matters: McKinsey's 2026 Banking Annual Review quantifies the strategic threat Adam's change programme must address: AI is not an efficiency lever but an existential attack on customer ownership, requiring HSBC WPB to rebuild its engagement model rather than simply automate existing processes.
bullets: McKinsey estimates banking profit pools could shrink 9–10% globally ($170bn) as AI agents remove the friction that historically kept customers with their banks — consumer deposits face 27% erosion, credit cards 34%

The risk is framed as banks becoming 'invisible infrastructure underneath somebody else's intelligent platform' — a direct threat to HSBC WPB's relationship-led model that Adam's programme must counter with stickiness-building change, not just operational efficiency

The McKinsey prescription is to 'rewire' — redesigning processes around unified data and cross-functional governance rather than layering AI onto legacy workflows — which maps directly onto WPB's change execution mandate for the remainder of 2026

source_links.title: The Finanser: Banks Richer, More Threatened

talking_points.headline: Citi's 4-Tool AI Adviser Stack Sets the 2026 RM Productivity Bar HSBC Must Beat
talking_points.why_it_matters: With Citi completing its adviser AI suite rollout in Q2 2026, Adam needs to benchmark HSBC WPB's RM tooling roadmap against a concrete competitive standard — Citi's full stack reportedly saves up to four hours per client meeting and will reach client-facing AI agents this summer.
bullets: Citi has deployed CitiScribe (AI meeting notes), Client 360 (unified client data view), and AskWealth CIO (live CIO insights via chat) across all North America wealth advisers in Q1–Q2 2026 — a comprehensive productivity suite with no known HSBC WPB equivalent yet live

Citi Sky — an AI-powered wealth 'team member' built with Google Cloud and Google DeepMind — enters Citigold this summer 2026, marking the shift from adviser tool to client-facing AI agent, a capability race HSBC WPB must enter

Simultaneously, DBS Hong Kong's Head of Investment & Advisory has moved to Standard Chartered — the talent arms race is accelerating in parallel with the technology race, compressing the window for HSBC to differentiate on advisory depth

source_links.title: PYMNTS: Citi Arms Advisors with 4 AI Tools

talking_points.headline: HSBC Stablecoin via PayMe Is an H2 2026 Change Delivery Commitment — Clock Is Running
talking_points.why_it_matters: HSBC's HKD stablecoin launch via PayMe in H2 2026 is an active product delivery commitment that requires change execution support this quarter — StanChart's HKDAP is already live, making slippage a first-mover risk.
bullets: HSBC received Hong Kong's first stablecoin licence from HKMA in April 2026 and is targeting H2 2026 launch via PayMe (3.3 million users), covering P2P payments, merchant transactions, and tokenised investment subscriptions — the product scope spans WPB, Retail, and Technology change teams

Standard Chartered's Anchorpoint JV (with HKT and Animoca Brands) has already begun phased HKDAP stablecoin rollout from Q2 2026 for institutional cross-border payments and tokenised settlement, establishing the first regulated HKD digital currency rail before HSBC's consumer product launches

This is a net-new product category requiring compliance infrastructure, AML controls, customer onboarding journeys, and operational processes that do not yet exist within WPB's current change portfolio — and must be delivered within six months

source_links.title: Hubbis: HSBC Stablecoin Launch Moves Ahead

AI & Technology.title: McKinsey 2026 Banking Review: Banks Richer Yet More Threatened by AI
AI & Technology.summary: McKinsey's 2026 Banking Annual Review finds banks at record profitability ($1.3T global net income in 2025) yet facing existential AI risk: profit pools could shrink 9-10% ($170bn) as agentic AI removes customer-retention friction. Consumer deposits face 27% erosion and credit card lending 34% if banks fail to rewire operating models.

AI & Technology.title: Fiserv Launches agentOS: Enterprise Agentic AI OS for Banking, Available August 2026
AI & Technology.summary: Fiserv launched agentOS on 14 May, the banking industry's first enterprise agentic AI platform, built on Amazon Bedrock and co-developed with OpenAI. Six banks are co-developing the platform with two in live beta; full availability is expected August 2026. Initial agents target commercial loan onboarding, AML triage analysis, and deposit intelligence — with policy enforcement, auditability, and human oversight embedded by design.

Competitor Intelligence.title: Citi Wealth Deploys 4 AI Tools for Advisers; Sky Agent Targets Citigold This Summer
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Citi Wealth has deployed CitiScribe (AI meeting notes, Q1 2026 NA rollout), Client 360 (unified client data platform, Q2 2026), and AskWealth CIO (CIO insights via chat interface) for all wealth advisers. Citi Sky — an AI-powered wealth team member built with Google Cloud and DeepMind — launches for Citigold clients this summer, completing the most comprehensive adviser AI stack deployed by any wealth manager.

Competitor Intelligence.title: UBS Wins Euromoney Asia Best International Private Bank for Fifth Consecutive Year
Competitor Intelligence.summary: Euromoney awarded UBS Asia's Best International Private Bank for the fifth consecutive year, recognising $383bn APAC AUM, 1,037 front-line bankers, and best-in-class CIO capabilities. UBS also won Asia's Best CIO 2026. The sustained recognition highlights how HSBC's international private bank must strengthen its advisory depth to compete with UBS's entrenched APAC leadership.

Competitor Intelligence.title: DBS Hong Kong Head of Investment & Advisory Defects to Standard Chartered
Competitor Intelligence.summary: DBS Hong Kong's Head of Investment and Advisory has moved to Standard Chartered, extending the talent war between two of APAC's most aggressive private banking rivals. The move follows StanChart's recent Greater China and North Asia leadership restructuring and accelerating APAC wealth hiring programme.

Operations & Change.title: 50% of Banks Creating AI Supervision Roles as Agentic Scale Becomes Priority
Operations & Change.summary: Nearly half of global banks and insurers are creating dedicated AI agent supervision roles as the industry transitions from agentic pilots to enterprise deployment. Despite 99% of firms planning to deploy agents, only 11% have done so, exposing governance and accountability gaps that senior executives must resolve before the August 2026 wave of new platform launches.

HSBC News.title: HSBC & StanChart Win HK's First Stablecoin Licences; H2 2026 Launch Now Imminent
HSBC News.summary: HKMA granted Hong Kong's first stablecoin licences to HSBC and Standard Chartered's Anchorpoint JV (with HKT and Animoca Brands). HSBC's HKD stablecoin launches H2 2026 via PayMe (3.3M users) covering P2P, merchant payments, and tokenised investment subscriptions. StanChart's HKDAP is already in phased Q2 2026 rollout targeting institutional cross-border payments and tokenised settlement.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Private Bank Q2 2026 Outlook: Asia at Heart of AI Supply Chain
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Private Bank's Q2 2026 investment outlook 'Changing Narratives, Continued Opportunity' identifies Asia as sitting at the heart of the global AI supply chain, supported by data-centre build-outs and leading tech and advanced manufacturing franchises. The bank advises clients to build resilience through broad diversification into Asia's innovation and income opportunities rather than timing market re-entries.

HSBC News.title: HSBC Private Bank Appoints Gautam Anand as Head of Global India
HSBC News.summary: HSBC Private Bank appointed Gautam Anand as Head of Global India effective 24 April 2026, leading coverage across India, Dubai, Hong Kong, Singapore and the UK. Anand joined HSBC in December 2023 and brings 25+ years of private banking and investment banking experience. The appointment creates a new global coordination node for HSBC's rapidly expanding India wealth franchise.

Private Banking & Wealth.title: APAC Wealth Market Hits $29.55T in 2026; India Fastest at 12.3% CAGR to 2031
Private Banking & Wealth.summary: APAC wealth management stands at $29.55T in 2026 and is projected to reach $41.82T by 2031 (7.18% CAGR). India is the fastest-growing market at 12.27% CAGR, with India's billionaire population expected to surge 51% from 207 to 313 by 2031. China contributes 47.85% of regional revenue; fintech advisors grow at 15.74% CAGR, outpacing traditional private banks.
